rationale: Both candidates correctly identify PR #4792 as the fix (None vs per_req_payloads starving the downstream stage) and both quote the exact code/comment that appears in the ground-truth thread, so core correctness is essentially tied and matches the resolution. X is more thoroughly grounded — it traces the full mechanism (_FULL_PAYLOAD_INPUT_STAGES registration, accumulate_full_payload_output gating, coordinator/scheduler timeout log lines, 300s→600s timeout change) across multiple files with distinct, plausible variable names per file, and closes out loose ends in the reporter's environment (NaN-clamp patch, empty-audio note) that Y ignores. Y's answer is substantively similar but is delivered as a messy, duplicated raw run-report (draft_answer repeated twice, followed by an internal diagnostics/JSON dump) rather than a clean maintainer-style post, hurting completeness and polish despite correct content.
